column1: ACTIVATION TIMELINE FORECAST — Metro Clinical Research Center (Academic)

column3: On critical path in 62% of simulations. Academic sites: median 88 days driven by institutional legal review, budget committee approval cycles, and indemnification negotiations.
Central IRB reduces variability. On critical path primarily when contract completes early and IRB backlog exists.
7-role certification requirement extends timeline when contract/IRB complete simultaneously.
Regulatory prep and SIV rarely on critical path with experienced sites.

column1: Rank_1: Contract Negotiation at Academic Sites
Probability: 62% of iterations cause >2 week delay
Impact: Median +35 days to total timeline

Mitigation: Pre-negotiate master CTA with institutional template. Engage legal 4 weeks before site selection. Use Florence pre-executed CTA library for budget templates. Historical data shows pre-negotiated CTAs reduce median contract time from 88 to 25 days.
Impact: Median +52 days to enrollment completion

Mitigation: Implement pre-screening protocol using EHR inclusion criteria check. Target sites with established NSCLC patient populations. Consider expanding PD-L1 stratification to reduce screen failure from biomarker mismatch.
Impact: Median +18 days due to productivity degradation

Mitigation: Assign dedicated coordinator for ONK-301 at high-enrollment sites. Maria Rodriguez currently manages 4 studies with 22 patients — within capacity but will hit threshold at 30 patients. Plan for backup coordinator assignment at enrollment milestone 15.
